[Generally guests that are not related to the departed person
are not invited to ^raddhas. However, great personages and saint-
ly persons, who may visit a house by chance on such an occasion,
are honoured and fed before the food is offered to the manes. The
word means that one purifies the 'entire row ol eaters',
though the row may contain some unholy persons and the food
served may not be quite holy and good.]

20 Bfhat Samhitd
n^u
The Yavanas are, no doubt, non-Aryan in origin. Still this
Science has found a stable position in them. When even they are
honoured as Sages, how much more should a Brahmin astrologer
be ?
[The realm of the intellect and spirit knows no barriers of
time and clime as well as of religion and colour. Our author dis-
plays his catholicity by acknowledging the greatness of Greek astro-
logy. This statement, however, does not prove that Indian astro-
logy is derived from the Greek. It only shows that by the time of
